datDb.datIntro: The DAT database is a free catalog of verified game dumps keyed by their file hashes (CRC32, MD5, SHA1) √¢‚Ç¨‚Äù sourced from No-Intro (cartridges), Redump (discs) and MAME (arcade) via the libretro-database project. GameHub downloads the DAT files once and imports them into a local database, so it can match your ROMs to their exact canonical names by hash √¢‚Ç¨‚Äù which fixes renamed or oddly-titled dumps and makes downloadable art (named to these same sets) line up 1:1. Fully offline afterward; re-import any time to pull in newer sets.
datDb.categoriesNote: Escolha uma ou mais categorias; os conjuntos sobrep√µem em prioridade do topo.
datDb.datFormatNote: Formatos suportados: XML No-Intro, .dat cl√°sico, ou simples .txt (um nome de jogo/linha).
datDb.customDatIntro: Envie seus pr√≥prios arquivos .dat (XML No-Intro, cl√°sico .dat ou .txt) para adicionar jogos personalizados e melhorar a correspond√™ncia de hashes.
datDb.offlineHashDb: Banco de dados de hashes offline ‚Äî nenhuma chamada de rede

setIntegrity.auditDesc: Classifica todos os ROMs com hash. {count, plural, one {# sistema tem} other {# sistemas t√™m}} cobertura DAT.

launchBox.aboutBefore: The LaunchBox Games Database is a free, community-maintained catalog of over 130,000 games across virtually every console and computer platform, with box art, clear logos, fan art, screenshots, developers, publishers, genres, release dates, ratings and descriptions. GameHub downloads the full
launchBox.aboutAfter: once (~500¬ÝMB) and imports it into a local database, so scraping from it afterward is completely offline ‚Äî no API keys, no accounts, no rate limits, and much faster than the online providers. It's the best first source to enable: re-import any time to pull in the latest community additions.
